name: Peanut Stuffed Chicken Breast
recipeInstructions:
1. Bone chicken breasts if needed, keeping meat as nearly intact as possible.
It's much easier to rip the meat off the bones than any knifework I've tried.
2. Pull off shreds and gobbets.
3. In a bowl, mix together peanut butter, honey, cumin, and dried mint.
Microwaving for 30 seconds or so thins it and makes it easier to mix.
4. Spread about 2 teaspoons of the peanut butter mixture in a thin layer over each piece of breast meat.
5. Place 1/12 of the shreds and gobbets on the breast and roll them up into a packet.
Tie the packet shut with butcher's twine or fasten with a toothpick.
6. Arrange the twelve packets in layers in the crock pot, being careful not to let any fall open.
7. Stir water into the bowl with any remaining peanut butter and mix well.
Add the packet of bouillon and pour over the chicken packets.
8. Add water as needed to cover the packets to a depth of at least one inch.
9. Turn off the crock pot and let it heat for 5 or 6 hours on high.
10. Remove packets from the pot and let drain on a platter.
The chicken fat will have migrated to the top of the liquid, which could be used as a flavoring for soups and other dishes.
